result.summary: Cloud Data Fusion exposes a Google Cloud REST management API on `datafusion.googleapis.com` plus `gcloud beta data-fusion` CLI commands; both use Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 credentials.
result.description: Cloud Data Fusion is Google Cloud’s managed data integration service for building and operating ETL/ELT data pipelines. It lets users create and manage Data Fusion instances and related resources in Google Cloud.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud access token
gcp_oauth.setup: Use a Google Cloud account with the Cloud Data Fusion API enabled for your project. The easiest path is to install the [Google Cloud CLI](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install), then run [`gcloud auth login`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/login) for user credentials or [`gcloud auth application-default login`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/application-default/login) for application default credentials. For server-to-server access, create a service account in the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/serviceaccounts), grant it the needed IAM roles for Cloud Data Fusion, and mint OAuth 2.0 access tokens with Google client libraries or `gcloud`.

surfaces.name: Cloud Data Fusion REST API

variables.name: project
variables.description: Google Cloud project ID in resource names like `projects/{project}/locations/{location}`.

variables.name: location
variables.description: Google Cloud region in resource names like `projects/{project}/locations/{location}`.

surfaces.notes: REST reference pages show methods under both `v1` and `v1beta1` on `datafusion.googleapis.com`, e.g. `GET https://datafusion.googleapis.com/v1/{name=projects/*/locations/*}`. The Go client exposes both gRPC and REST clients for the same API. No machine-readable OpenAPI document was found in the public docs.

surfaces.name: gcloud beta data-fusion
surfaces.notes: Cloud Data Fusion management commands are exposed under the `gcloud beta data-fusion` command group, including instance create/delete/describe/list and IAM operations.
